landing.title: Bright Boost
landing.subtitle: Learn. Play. Explore!
landing.tagline: A bilingual K-8 STEM learning platform that builds strong foundations and prepares students for advanced technology pathways in AI, quantum, and biotech.
landing.demoPath: Recommended path: Log in as teacher → Create a class → Open Showcase Mode → Log in as student → Join the class → Play a module.

reviewers.title: BrightBoost Evaluator Guide
reviewers.subtitle: Everything you need to explore, evaluate, and share feedback on BrightBoost — a bilingual K-8 STEM learning platform that builds strong foundations and prepares students for advanced technology pathways.

whatIs.title: What is BrightBoost?
whatIs.body: BrightBoost is a bilingual (English/Spanish) K-8 STEM learning platform that builds strong foundations and prepares students for advanced technology pathways in AI, quantum, and biotech. The platform features tiered pathways for K-2, 3-5, and 6-8. The current rollout begins with K-2 content — turning foundational skills like sequencing, patterns, and problem-solving into interactive game-based adventures. Teachers create classes, assign modules, and track student progress. Students play through activities, earn XP, and build confidence. Grades 3-5 and 6-8 are planned in future releases.

explore.title: How to Explore This Demo
explore.step1Desc: A ready-made presentation showing what students learn, sample achievements, and family take-home activities. No login required.
explore.step2Desc: Log in with the demo teacher account to see class management, resources, the prep checklist, and the PD Hub.
explore.step3Desc: Log in with the demo student account to play through a module, earn XP, and see the avatar system.

teachers.title: What Teachers Can Do
teachers.item1: Create classes and generate join codes for students
teachers.item2: Assign STEM modules with built-in prep checklists
teachers.item3: Track individual and class-wide progress in real time
teachers.item5: Run a Family STEAM Night showcase from the dashboard
teachers.item6: Participate in professional development sessions and reflections

students.title: What Students Experience
students.item1: Join a class with a simple code — early learners (K-2) use icon + PIN login, no passwords needed
students.item2: Play through sequencing, pattern, and problem-solving activities
students.item3: Earn XP, level up, and customize a robot avatar
students.item4: See personal goals and streaks on a kid-friendly dashboard
students.item5: Use the platform in English or Spanish

lookFor.title: What to Look For as a Partner
lookFor.intro: As you explore, here are four lenses that help evaluate BrightBoost for your context:
lookFor.engagementDesc: Are the current K-2 activities age-appropriate and motivating? Does the XP/avatar system feel rewarding? Can you see this scaling to 3-5 and 6-8?
lookFor.teacherUxDesc: Is it easy to create a class, assign work, and check progress? Does the prep checklist help with lesson planning?
lookFor.accessibilityDesc: Does the icon-based login work for pre-readers? Is the bilingual support effective? Does it load on Chromebooks?
lookFor.trustDesc: Is it clear what data is collected? Does the privacy policy address your concerns? Is the audit trail visible?

privacy.title: Privacy & Trust
privacy.item1: BrightBoost collects only learning progress data — no ads, no third-party tracking
privacy.item2: COPPA-aware design: early learner accounts (K-2) don't require email or passwords
privacy.item3: All data can be exported or deleted at the end of a pilot
privacy.item4: Audit logging tracks login, signup, and password reset events

reviewers.ctaPrompt: Ready to see it in action?
reviewers.footer: BrightBoost is a project of the Bright Bots Initiative. Questions? Use the feedback button inside the app or contact your pilot coordinator.

activityPlayer.quizSubtitle: Answer questions to complete the lesson!
activityPlayer.checkHints: Check the hints and try again!
activityPlayer.breakBody: Great work! You've finished 3 activities! Time to stretch, move around, or try something fun:
breakSuggestions: Draw a picture of what you just learned!
Tell someone nearby one cool thing you learned!
Stretch your arms up high like a rocket!
Look out the window and find something interesting!
Take 5 deep breaths — in through your nose, out through your mouth!

rhymeRide.title: Rhyme & Ride
rhymeRide.howToPlayDesc: Words scroll across the screen. Tap or click the word that rhymes with the prompt word shown at the top!
rhymeRide.objectiveDesc: Find the word that rhymes with the prompt word before time runs out!
rhymeRide.tip1: Look at the prompt word at the top of the screen
rhymeRide.tip2: Words will scroll across in different lanes
rhymeRide.tip3: Tap/click the word that rhymes with the prompt
rhymeRide.tip4: Avoid clicking wrong words - you'll lose a life!
rhymeRide.tipTricky: Watch out for tricky words that look similar but don't rhyme
rhymeRide.tipScroll: Don't let the correct answer scroll off-screen!

bounceBuds.title: Bounce & Buds
bounceBuds.howToPlayDesc: Use the paddle to bounce your Buddy through the correct gate! Read the clue and aim for the right answer.
bounceBuds.objectiveDesc: Bounce your Buddy ball through the gate that matches the clue!
bounceBuds.control1: Use arrow keys (Left/Right) or A/D to move the paddle
bounceBuds.control2: On mobile: drag the paddle left and right
bounceBuds.control3: Bounce the ball off your paddle to keep it in play
bounceBuds.score1: Hit the correct gate to score a point
bounceBuds.score2: Wrong gates or falling out costs a life
bounceBuds.score3: Build a streak for bonus points!
bounceBuds.tip1: Read the clue carefully before bouncing
bounceBuds.tip2: Use the obstacles to guide your ball
bounceBuds.tip3: Take your time - accuracy beats speed!

gotchaGears.title: Gotcha Gears
gotchaGears.howToPlayDesc: Read the clue, then catch the correct gear! Use arrow keys or tap the lanes to move your catcher.
gotchaGears.objectiveDesc: Catch the gear with the correct answer before it scrolls off screen!
gotchaGears.step1: Planning Phase: Read the clue and watch the gears appear
gotchaGears.step2: Action Phase: Move your catcher to the right lane
gotchaGears.step3: Use ↑/↓ arrow keys (or W/S) or tap a lane
gotchaGears.step4: Press CATCH or click when the gear reaches the catcher zone
gotchaGears.score2: Wrong catch = Try again (no life lost in kid mode)
gotchaGears.tip1: Watch for gears scrolling on all three lanes
gotchaGears.tip2: Position early and wait for the right moment!
gotchaGears.tip3: Speed increases each round - stay focused!

boost.s2: I want to bake a cake!
boost.s3: Oops… the steps are mixed up!
boost.s4: A plan with steps is called an algorithm.
boost.s5: Let's put the steps in the right order!

q1.hint: Look at the picture with the candles! 🎂

q2.prompt: A plan with steps is called…
q2.hint: It's a big word that starts with A... 📜

q3.hint: When something is broken, we have to ___ it. 🛠️

boost.reviewKeyIdea: An algorithm is steps in order. Debug means fix mistakes.

rhymo.s1: Hey there! I'm Rhymo the Rider!
rhymo.s2: Words that sound alike at the end are called rhymes.
rhymo.s3: Cat and hat rhyme because they both end in -at!
rhymo.s4: Can you find the word that rhymes? Let's ride and find out!

q1.hint: It sounds like cat but goes on your head! 🎩

q2.hint: You do this with your legs really fast! 🏃

q3.prompt: Words that rhyme sound the same at the…
q3.hint: Cat and hat both END with -at! 🔚

rhymo.reviewKeyIdea: Rhyming words sound the same at the end, like cat/hat and sun/run.

buddy.s1: Hi! I'm Buddy. I'm a Bio Explorer! 🌿🔬
buddy.s2: Living things are made of tiny parts called cells. 🧫
buddy.s3: Tiny helpers called microbes live in soil and help plants. 🦠
buddy.s4: We read clues and test ideas—like steps in code! ✅

q1.prompt: Tiny parts of living things are called…

q3.hint: Roots take in water from the ground. 💧

buddy.reviewKeyIdea: Cells and microbes are tiny parts of life. We read clues and test ideas like scientists!

gearbot.s1: Hi! I'm Gearbot. I help robots think! ⚙️🤖
gearbot.s2: Robots need a plan to solve problems. 📝
gearbot.s3: When robots make mistakes, we debug—that means fix! 🛠️
gearbot.s4: Practice helps robots learn, just like you! 🌟

q1.hint: Debug means to fix what went wrong! 🛠️

q3.prompt: A plan with steps is called a…
q3.hint: Rules tell us what to do step by step! 📜

gearbot.reviewKeyIdea: Robots use plans and rules. Debug means fix mistakes. Practice helps us learn!

r4.clue: Tiny living helpers in soil are ____.

r1.clue: The robot keeps making mistakes. What should it do?

r2.clue: We want the robot to learn. What helps most?

r3.clue: We need to sort things. What do we pick first?
r3.hint: A rule tells how to group things.

r4.clue: The robot needs steps to follow. What is that?
r4.hint: A plan is steps in order.

r5.clue: We look at clues to find an answer. That is called…

r6.clue: The robot tries something new. What is it doing?
r6.hint: Testing means trying to see if it works.

r7.clue: We teach the robot by giving it…
r7.hint: Data is information the robot uses to learn.

rhymeRide.title: Rhyme & Ride
rhymeRide.briefingStory: Rhymo is riding through three amazing worlds! Word cards are zooming down three lanes. Tap the lane with the rhyming word before it passes. Build combos for bonus points!
rhymeRide.tipTapLane: Tap the lane with the rhyming word
rhymeRide.tipShowdown: Watch for showdown rounds every 5 hits!
rhymeRide.showdownNotRhyme: Not a rhyme! Let it pass.
rhymeRide.showdownGoodEye: Good eye! That wasn't a rhyme.
rhymeRide.tooSlow: Too slow! Try to tap faster.
rhymeRide.keyboardHint: Press 1 2 3 or tap a lane | M mute

moveMeasure.title: Move, Measure & Improve
moveMeasure.introDesc: Test your body's superpowers! Dash, jump, and toss — then use data to improve.
moveMeasure.dashHint: Tap when the marker hits the green zone!
moveMeasure.jumpHint: Hold and release in the green zone!
moveMeasure.tossHint: Slide to set your angle, then release!
moveMeasure.exitQuestion: How do you know you improved?
moveMeasure.celebDesc: Great scientists measure and compare to learn.

skyShield.title: Sky Shield Patterns
skyShield.introDesc: Light drops are falling! Watch the pattern and place your shield to catch them.
skyShield.practiceHint: Move your shield to catch the light!
skyShield.scanHint: Tap Scan to reveal the color!
skyShield.exitQuestion: Blue, blue, gold, blue, blue, ___. What comes next?
skyShield.celebDesc: You watched, predicted, and chose the right shield.

fastLane.title: Fast Lane Signals
fastLane.introDesc: Deliver the science supplies safely! Read the signals and pick the best lane.
fastLane.practiceHint: Pick the lane with the green signal!
fastLane.signalsHint: Yellow means it might close soon!
fastLane.lookAheadHint: Plan for the NEXT signal too!
fastLane.celebDesc: You watched the signals and made safe choices.

qualifyTuneRace.title: Qualify, Tune, Race
qualifyTuneRace.introDesc: First we test. Then we tune. Then we race again!
qualifyTuneRace.qualifyHint: Steer around the cones. Drive carefully!
qualifyTuneRace.tuneHint: Change one thing to test it!
qualifyTuneRace.exitQuestion: To make a fair test, how many things should you change?
qualifyTuneRace.celebDesc: You changed one thing and measured the difference. That's how scientists work!
